LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RENKA PROP, LLC'S MOTION TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS NOW SUPPORTED BY A PROPER SUPPLEMENTAL AFFIDAVIT OF FAULT FROM ATTORNEY PAUL MANASIAN. THE MOTION WAS "MADE PURSUANT TO THE MANDATORY PROVISION" OF CCP 473(B) ON THE GROUNDS OF "ATTORNEY MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR NEGLECT." THUS, ATTORNEY MANASIAN SHALL PAY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL $10,000 IN "REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES AND COSTS." (SEE ID.) VERIFIED ANSWER IS TO BE FILED BY DECEMBER 21, 2018. MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert B. Kaplan, Esq., Jeffer & Mangels Butlwer & Mitchell LLP, 415-984-9673 for Plaintiff;
Paul Manasian, Esq., 415-730-3419 for Defendant;

RULING: ARGUED. Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Renka Prop, LLC's motion to vacate entry of default and default judgment is GRANTED. The motion is now supported by a proper supplemental affidavit of fault from attorney Paul Manasian. The motion was "made pursuant to the mandatory provision" of CCP 473(b) on the grounds of "attorney mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect."
